author: @ancapftw9113
text:
I assume you can N and P dope the ink to make diodes and transistors with it.  How would you go about doing that?  Could you just add small amounts of metal to the different inks?

My idea is to use this in a video game (though it could work it real life, and I want to go for maximum realism) as a cheap/easy way to manufacture electronics on Mars, where graphene is mass produced from the carbon in the air.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it would be a little more complicated than that but not by much - good luck with the game

author: @ElGatoLoco698
text: This gives me an idea.  How bout making homemade paper with the conducting paint included in the pulp.  So you'd have an entire conductive sheet of paper without painting it on. Then you could make the paper as thick or thin as you want and even cut it up in strips.  Just a thought.

author: @AnnayahUsman
text: What ink have you used? From where can it be purchased?

author: @joeglory425
text: Can this ink physically glue two pieces of charcoal together or two pieces of carbon fiber and if so will the connection remain stable if immersed in sulfuric acid or potassium hydroxide?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I haven't tried - but probably - yes

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: i'll try and find time to design some real circuits lol

author: @mrvos96
text: That's great :) I'm happy to hear that it's so durable. Congratulations for the design :)

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: i haven't really tested it - you can certainly bend it - but how many times before it cracks i have no idea - if i get the time and inclination to bend a piece backwards and forwards a few thousand times i'll let you know. lol

author: @mrvos96
text:
This is great, i love your channel alot.
How flexible is the ink, what i mean by that is how much can you bend the paper before the ink cracks.
Regards. mrvos

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: It is an ink of my own design

author: @Kyledzare20
text: Robert. What is using as conducting paint or ink? what is component ?

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hi, no it isn't. Graphene oxide doesn't conduct.

author: @enriqueomar1971
text: Hello paint you use is graphite oxide

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Well in a way you are quite right. But you are kind of jumping the gun and missing the point here. This is just about simple DC circuits that can be any design you like to light a lamp or series of lamps. If, for instance, you wanted to make a pretty Christmas card. Inductors and fractal aerials are something I plan on getting into later - one step at a time my friend

author: @arse124
text: If you made spirals  It would not be the same, it would  have an inductance provided you use AC source. In fact it would be nice and pretty easy to experiment with this ink with fractal structures of capacitor and inductors.
